title: Quick check: tree and Venn diagrams

questions.text: On a tree diagram, how do you combine probabilities for a single complete path from left to right?
questions.explanation: Each branch along a single path represents a conditional probability given everything before it, so by the multiplication rule you multiply along the path.

questions.text: A bag has 4 red and 6 green counters (10 total). Two are drawn without replacement. Find the probability both are red.
questions.explanation: $P(R_1) = \dfrac{4}{10}$, and after removing one red, $P(R_2 \mid R_1) = \dfrac{3}{9}$. So $P(R_1 \cap R_2) = \dfrac{4}{10}\times\dfrac{3}{9} = \dfrac{12}{90} = \dfrac{2}{15} \approx 0.1333$.
